content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

June 20, 2017

Nancy Diamond
General Counsel
Redwood Coast Energy Authority
822 G Street, Suite 3
Arcata, CA 95521

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-17-105

Dear Ms. Diamond:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹ and Section 1090. The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 83114(b); Regulation 18329(a).) However, the Commission may decline to provide written advice if it would be appropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(F).)

Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(b)(7).) After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. Your current question involves an analysis of one of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred. Issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by Section 83111.

Please note that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your request. In addition, nothing in the Act prevents you from seeking legal advice from private counsel.

Sincerely,

Jack C. Woodside
General Counsel

By: [signature]
Zachary W. Norton
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

ZWN:jgl

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide written advice to Nancy Diamond of the Redwood Coast Energy Authority regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act and Section 1090, citing that the analysis involves past conduct and interrelated decisions already made.
